_comment: Overlay drop to /etc/claude-code/managed-settings.d/20-team-brain.json (owner: root). Claude Code COMBINES hooks across all settings sources, so this adds the team-brain hooks org-wide WITHOUT touching any user's ~/.claude/settings.json. autoMemoryEnabled:false disables Claude's native file-based memory fleet-wide (users can't re-enable) so team-brain is THE memory system. The hooks are subcommands of brain.py; each guards against BRAIN_DISTILLER recursion. The interactive memory surface is the agentmemory MCP (see managed-mcp.json); the permissions.allow list below auto-approves the SAFE memory tools (read + reversible curation) so they run without prompts, while leaving destructive ops (memory_governance_delete), local-file writers, and the agent-workflow tools to PROMPT (omitted = prompt; we intentionally do NOT use allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly, so users keep their own non-MCP allow rules).
